title: Transition Services for Students with Disabilities - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Transition Services for Students with Disabilities 21 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Blindness Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness MALHEUR EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SPECIAL EDUCATION SERVICES AND PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Student Services/Special Education Department at Malheur Education Service District Region 14 helps school districts support students with unique learning needs. Referral, diagnosis, consultation, and direct treatment therapy services are provided. 363 A Street W Vale, OR 97918 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 473-4821 Get needs Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm during the school year Email (541) 461-8200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN BENTON LINCOLN EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SPECIAL EDUCATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for children with vision impairment, hearing impairment, severe orthopedic impairment, and autism spectrum disorder. Collaborates with component school districts and programs to provide specialized support. Services includes consultation, assessment, instruction, and adaptive or specialized materials and equipment, as well as assistance with IEP development and implementation. 905 4th Avenue SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Must contact school's special education director to start the referral process and determine eligibility. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 812-2770 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SPECIAL EDUCATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of services for children with special needs and the school districts serving them, including IEP support and transition services. 11611 NE Ainsworth Circle Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Children with special needs Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 257-1679 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND EUGENE FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults. Also provides independent living services for visually impaired individuals. Includes orientation and mobility training, techniques of daily living, Braille, counseling, support groups, vocational training, job development, and transition services for blind high school students. Also offers a selection of adaptive items for people with visual impairments. 541 Willamette Avenue #408 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by service; adults certified legally blind or diagnosed with condition leading to blindness, or age 55 years and above and unable to drive or read newspaper with glasses. Age 55 and older for independent living services. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 686-7990 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND MEDFORD FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 378-8478 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COAST EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SPECIAL EDUCATION SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of services for children with special needs, and the school districts serving them, including IEP support and transition services. Also offers Augmentative and Alternative Communication, a Feeding Team, a Crisis Prevention Institute, and a Special Education Director. 1350 Teakwood Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 269-1611 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHERN OREGON EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides evaluative, consultative, and direct special education services to students with disabilities, including assistive technology, audiological, autism, deaf and hard of hearing, long-term care and treatment, visual, psychological, orthopedic, speech and language, and STEPS (students with severe and/or multiple disabilities). Also provides evaluation and special education services to children from birth to years. 101 N Grape Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 776-8590 3110 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WILLAMETTE EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT WILLAMETTE ESD MARION CENTER SPECIAL EDUCATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers direct instruction and related services to children and students with special needs, their families and school districts. 2611 Pringle Road SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Special education programs provide instruction to students in grades K-12, ages 5-21. Referral and placement decisions are based on recommendations by the IEP team, which includes the child's family.
